assessment.summary_for_seller: Good run: you held context, answered on-point, and brought the conversation to a next step.

criteria.name: Contextual first contact
criteria.short_reason: First message anchors to a specific signal from the card.
criteria.evidence_quote: David, you've already seen Westline's fragile mix of intermediaries and manual exceptions. Mellow provides a structured alternative: CoR with a documented control zone, audit trail on every payment. No promises on what's outside our scope. What would you call the critical weakness in the current scheme from a defensibility standpoint?
criteria.label: Contextual opening

criteria.name: Compliance and boundary of responsibility
criteria.short_reason: Seller stated Mellow's control scope without overclaiming.

criteria.name: Handling core objections
criteria.short_reason: At least two key objections addressed substantively.
criteria.evidence_quote: David, you've already seen Westline's fragile mix of intermediaries and manual exceptions. Mellow provides a structured alternative: CoR with a documented control zone, audit trail on every payment. No promises on what's outside our scope. What would you call the critical weakness in the current scheme from a defensibility standpoint?

objections.objection: That's careful. But I need three things: what you take on, what you don't, how it's substantiated. Not declarations. Без overclaim.
Defensibility requires no grey zones. Show me exactly where your responsibility ends and ours begins.
Where exactly does your zone end and ours begin? Who owns the gap between Mellow, our legal, and client? Без overclaim.
The grey zone question: when there's an overlap between your scope and ours — who owns that? What's the documented answer?

criteria.label: Core objection handling

transcript.text: David, you've already seen Westline's fragile mix of intermediaries and manual exceptions. Mellow provides a structured alternative: CoR with a documented control zone, audit trail on every payment. No promises on what's outside our scope. What would you call the critical weakness in the current scheme from a defensibility standpoint?
That's careful. But I need three things: what you take on, what you don't, how it's substantiated. Not declarations. Без overclaim.
The boundary is explicit: our scope covers the payment chain, KYC and contractor documentation. Your legal and finance teams own the strategic decisions. The overlap is mapped — not assumed.
Defensibility requires no grey zones. Show me exactly where your responsibility ends and ours begins.
